authors_affiliation: Deepankar Nankani, Pallabi Saikia, Rashmi Dutta Baruah; Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, Assam, India

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (full test set); paper also reports scores on a 20% holdout validation split (0.305) and stratified 10-fold cross-validation on training data (0.21±0.04)

self_reported_metrics.other: Full test set AUPRC 0.229. Additional splits reported: validation data (AUROC 0.825, AUPRC 0.326, Accuracy 0.331, F-measure 0.286, Challenge score 0.305) and three partial 'Test Data' subsets (challenge scores 0.648, 0.25, -0.287).

model.architecture_details: 8-layer 1D convolutional ResNet: 1-D convolution, batch normalization, dropout, max pooling, and skip connections; 64 filters of size 16x1, stride 1; final dense layer of 27 neurons with softmax. Weights initialized with Xavier initialization; ReLU activations; dropout probability 0.5.
model.key_novelty: Systematically benchmarks 17 deep learning architectures (CNN/ResNet, RNN variants (LSTM/GRU/BiLSTM/BiGRU), CNN+RNN hybrids, and RNN/hybrid models with an attention mechanism) for concurrent arrhythmia classification, showing a simple 8-layer ResNet with skip connections outperforms far more complex recurrent and attention-augmented models while training orders of magnitude faster.

input.signal_length_window: 2500-sample fixed-length segments extracted from each record (original record lengths ranged from 2500 to 462600 samples)
input.preprocessing: Records segmented into 2500-sample windows and labelled per the record's diagnosis; only the first label of each multi-label record was used for training and only a single class predicted per record at test time; at inference, predictions across all segments of a record were averaged and the most frequently predicted class chosen.

data.training_datasets: All six official Challenge 2020 training databases (PTB-XL, PTBDB, INCART, CPSC2018, CPSC-Extra, Georgia DB); no external data

training.cross_validation_strategy: Main dataset split 80% training / 20% validation for hyperparameter tuning; final ResNet model additionally evaluated with stratified 10-fold cross-validation; early stopping on validation accuracy (patience 5 epochs)
training.hardware: Intel Xeon processor, 32 GB RAM, NVIDIA GeForce Titan Xp GPU with 12 GB GDDR6 VRAM; batch-wise training (batch size 16) via Keras generator to avoid loading full dataset into memory

contribution.summary: Benchmarks 17 deep learning architectures (CNN, RNN, CNN+RNN hybrids, and attention-augmented variants) for 12-lead ECG arrhythmia classification and shows that a simple 8-layer 1D ResNet outperforms substantially more complex RNN/attention-based and hybrid models on both challenge score and training time.
